title: The money multiplier and the expansion of the money supply
description: Practice what you have learned about the money multiplier in this exericse, including calculating the simple money multiplier and applying the money multiplier to changes in the money supply
topic_path: Banking and the expansion of the money supply
The money multiplier and the expansion of the money supply

assessment_items.question_content:
The nation of Kocherlakota has a money supply equal to $\$10,000$ and a monetary base equal to $\$2{,}500$.

**What is the money multiplier in Kocherlakota?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: This cannot be determined without knowing the reserve requirement

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following leads to an increase in the money supply?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in loans made from excess reserves
An increase in the reserve requirement
An increase in deposits in a bank
A bank purchase of an asset, such as a bond
An increase in the amount of bonds that a bank is holding

assessment_items.question_content:
**If the money supply is equal to $\$50{,}000$ and the money multiplier is $2$, what is the size of the monetary base?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: This cannot be determined with the information provided.

assessment_items.question_content:
The Central Bank of Westeros increased the monetary base by $20\%$. As a result, the money supply increased by $60\%$. 

**What is the money multiplier in Westeros?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: This cannot be determined without knowing the reserve requirement.

assessment_items.question_content:
Excess reserves in Gondor Bank have increased by $\$1000$.

**Which of the following measures will tell you the size of the expansion of the money supply that will occur as a result of this increase in excess reserves?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following best describes the use of the money multiplier $(\dfrac{1}{rr})$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To determine the size of the change in the money supply as a result of a change in the monetary base
To determine how much a bank’s excess reserves will change as a result of a new bank deposit
To determine how much a bank’s required reserves will change as a result of a new bank deposit
To determine the  difference between the actual expansion of the money supply and the predicted expansion of the money supply
To determine the final impact that an increase in government spending will have on aggregate demand

assessment_items.question_content:
The current balance sheet for the Third Bank of Westeros is shown below. The reserve requirement in Westeros is 10%.

Assets | Liabilities
-|-
Reserves: $\$2{,}000$ | Loans: $\$18{,}000$
Demand deposits: $\$20{,}000$ | Owners’ equity: $\$0$


Ygritte deposits $\$1{,}000$ into her account at the Third Bank of Westeros. 

**What is the maximum dollar amount of new loans that can be created throughout the banking system as a result of Ygritte’s deposit?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
In general, the maximum amount of new loans that can be made from a deposit can be calculated using the following formula:

Maximum new loans that can be made after a new deposit = Excess reserves from deposit x Money multiplier
Based on Ygritte’s deposit and a reserve requirement of $10\%$, you can calculate the maximum new loans as follows:

$\begin{align}\text{Maximum new loans that can be made after a new deposit} &= (\text{Deposit} - \text{Required Reserves}) \times \text{Money multiplier}\\\\
=(\$1000 - \$100) x \dfrac{1}{10\%}\\\\
=\$900 x 10\\\\
=\$9000\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume that all banks fully loan out excess reserves and all money is re-deposited into banks.

**If the money multiplier is $4$, what is the reserve requirement?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following calculations would be used predict the maximum increase in the money supply that would result from an increase in the monetary base?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**The size of the expansion of the money supply depends on which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The change in the tax rate

assessment_items.question_content:
The balance sheet of the First Bank of Cloud City is below. The reserve requirement is 25%.


Assets | Liabilities
-|-
Reserves: \$21,000 |Demand deposits: \$80,000
Loans: \$59,000 | Owners’ equity: \$0

**What is the maximum change in new loans that can be created throughout the banking system based on this balance sheet?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Loans can increase by up to \$4,000.
Loans can decrease by \$4,000 at most.
Loans can increase by \$21,000 at most.
Loans can decrease by \$21,000 at most.
Loans cannot change; only the money supply can change.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following  best describes why the  expansion of the money supply might be different than is predicted by the simple money multiplier?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Banks may choose to keep excess reserves instead of making loans
People keep all their money deposited in banks
The marginal propensity to consume decreases
The marginal propensity to save increases
The central bank lowers the reserve requirement

assessment_items.question_content:
The reserve ratio in Cloud City is 25%. When Lando purchased a bond from the bank in the amount of \$100, the money supply increased by \$300.

**Which of the following is the most likely reason the money supply increased by \$300 instead of \$400?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The citizens of Cloud City keep their money under their mattresses instead of in banks.
Banks refuse to hold excess reserves.
The spending multiplier is larger than the tax multiplier.
People store their wealth in bonds instead of in cash.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios would cause the money multiplier to be smaller than predicted by the calculation below?**

 $\dfrac{1}{\text{reserve ratio}}$

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: People keep all of their money in banks instead of holding cash.

assessment_items.question_content:
A simplified version of the balance sheet for the Bank of Kocherlakota is shown below.

Assets | Liabilities
-|-
Total reserves $\$5000$ | Deposits $\$6000$
Loans $\$1000$ | Owners equity $\$0$

**If the reserve requirement is $10\%$, what is the dollar value of new loans that this bank can make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To find the dollar value of new loans that a bank can make, you can apply this equation:

$\text{Excess reserves available to loan out} = \text{Total reserves} - \text{required reserves}$

Required reserves are found by multiplying the value of deposits by the reserve requirement. So, this equation becomes:

$\begin{align}\text{Excess reserves available to loan out} &= \text{Total reserves} - \text{required reserves}\\\\
& = \text{Total reserves} - (rr \times \text{deposits})\end{align}$

The bank currently has $\$5000$ in total reserves. If the reserve requirement is $10\%$, and there are $\$6000$ in deposits, the bank must keep $\$600$ in required reserves:

$\begin{align}\text{Excess reserves available to loan out} &= \text{Total reserves} - \text{required reserves}\\\\
& = \text{Total reserves} - (rr \times \text{deposits})\\\\
&=\$5000 - (0.1 \times \$6000)\\\\
&=\$5000 - \$600\end{align}$

The bank can make $\$4400$ in new loans:

$\begin{align}
\text{Excess reserves available to loan out} &=\$5000 - (0.1 \times \$6000)\\\\
&=\$5000 - \$600\\\\
&=\$4400\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
The current balance sheet of the Iron Bank is given below. The reserve requirement is $20\%$.

Assets | Liabilities
-|-
Reserves: $\$4{,}000$|Demand deposits: $\$20{,}000$
Loans: $\$16{,}000$ |Owners’ equity: $\$0$

**If Cersei deposits $\$100$ in the Iron Bank, what will be the dollar value of new loans that the Iron Bank can make?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Banks make loans out of excess reserves. To determine the amount of new loans that a bank can make after a new deposit, add together the existing excess reserves and the new excess reserves:

$\text{Amount of new loans} = \text{Amount of preexisting excess reserves }+ \text{New excess reserves}$
Before the new deposit, the amount of preexisting excess reserves is $\$0$:

$\begin{align}\text{Preexisting excess reserves} &= \text{Existing total reserves} - \text{Existing required reserves}\\\\
&=\$4{,}000 - (20\% \times \$20{,}000)\\\\
&=\$4{,}000 - \$4{,}000\\\\
&=\$0\end{align}$

Next, you need to find how excess reserves change as the result of an increase in deposits:

$\begin{align}\text{New excess reserves} &= \text{Deposit} -( \text{Reserve requirement} \times \text{Deposit})\\\\
&=\$100 - (20\% \times \$100)\\\\
&=\$100 - \$20\\\\
&=\$80\end{align}$
After the new deposit is made, add the existing excess reserves to the new excess reserves to find the total amount of excess reserves the bank will have after the deposit:

$\begin{align}\text{Total new excess reserves} &= \text{Existing excess reserve} + \text{New excess reserves}
&=\$0 + \$80\\\\
&=\$80\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
The current balance sheet of the Iron Bank is given below. The reserve requirement is 10%.


Assets| Liabilities
-|-
Reserves: $\$3{,}000$ | Loans: $\$22{,}000$
Demand deposits: $\$25{,}000$ | Owners’ equity: $\$0$

**If Hodor deposits $\$100$ in the Iron Bank, what will be the dollar value of new loans that the Iron Bank can make?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Banks make loans out of excess reserves. To determine the amount of new loans that a bank can make after a new deposit, add together the existing excess reserves and the new excess reserves:

$\text{Amount of new loans} = \text{Amount of preexisting excess reserves }+ \text{New excess reserves}$
Before the new deposit is made, the amount of preexisting excess reserves is $\$500$:

$\begin{align}\text{Preexisting excess reserves} &= \text{Existing total reserves} - \text{Existing required reserves}\\\\
&=\$3{,}000 - (10\% \times \$25{,}000)\\\\
&=\$3{,}000 - \$2{,}500\\\\
&=\$500\end{align}$
Next, you need to find how excess reserves change as the result of the new deposit:

$\begin{align}\text{New excess reserves} &= \text{Deposit} - (\text{Reserve requirement} \times \text{Deposit})\\\\
&=\$100 - (10\% \times \$100)\\\\
&=\$100 - \$10\\\\
&=\$90\end{align}$
After the new deposit is made, add the preexisting excess reserves to the new excess reserves to find the total amount of excess reserves the bank will have after the deposit:

$\begin{align}\text{Total new excess reserves} &= \text{Preexisting excess reserve} + \text{New excess reserves}\\\\
&=\$500 + \$90\\\\
&=\$590\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
The Bank of Narayana is one of many banks in the financial system in the nation of Kocherlakota. Rai deposits $\$1000$ in the Bank of Narayana. The reserve requirement in Kocherlakota is $20\%$.

**How much in new loans can this bank make as a result of this deposit?**

Werner gets paid $\$10{,}000$ for coordinating a large construction project and deposits that money in Mesa Verde bank. 

**How much in new loans can the bank make as a result of the deposit?**

Pancake Bank has $\$10{,}000$ in required reserves and $\$50{,}000$ in excess reserves. The bank is required to keep $20\%$ of all deposits in reserve.

**If Chinhui deposits $\$5{,}000$ into Pancake Bank, how much in new loans can the bank make as a result of the deposit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

First National Bank is one of many banks in a banking system. It has no excess reserves, and the reserve requirement is $50\%$. It has $\$20{,}000$ in bonds.

**How much in new loans can the bank make if the central bank buys the bank's bonds?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Tahani Bank is one of many banks in a banking system. The required reserve ratio is 25%, and the balance sheet for Tahani Bank is shown here:

Assets | Liabilities & Equity
-|-
Loans: $\$50{,}000$ |Deposits: $\$80{,}000$ 
Total reserves: $\$30{,}000$ | Owner's equity: $\$0$

Michael deposits $\$4{,}000$ in the bank. 

**How much in new loans can this bank make if it decides to fully loan out?**

Assets | Liabilities & Equity
-|-

Total reserves: $\$10{,}000$ | Owner’s equity: $\$30{,}000$
Bonds: $\$20{,}000$ |
Total Assets: $\$130{,}000$ | $\$130{,}000$

**If Vizzini deposits $\$5{,}000$ into a demand deposit at Rous Bank, what is the maximum change in demand deposits in this banking system as a result of that deposit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Cal Bank keeps no excess reserves and has a reserve requirement of $20\%$. Assume all money is held in banks. Professor Telphy has sold off his collection of drones for $\$8{,}000$ and deposits it into Cal Bank.

**What is the maximum change in demand deposits in the banking system as a result of this deposit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Vivek wins $\$20{,}000$ in a rap battle contest and deposits it into First Bank. The bank has a $25\%$ reserve requirement and keeps no excess reserves. Assume all money is held in banks.

**What is the maximum change in demand deposits in the banking system as a result of this deposit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Zoey earns $\$10{,}000$ from a lemonade stand and deposits it into Cal Bank. The bank has a $50\%$ reserve requirement and keeps no excess reserves. Assume all money is held in banks.

**What is the maximum change in demand deposits in the banking system as a result of this deposit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Assume a fractional reserve banking system has a reserve requirement of $5\%$, all money is held in banks, and banks keep no excess reserves. 

**Which of the following best describes why the maximum amount of loans that can be made when a bank sells $\$100$ in bonds is greater than when $\$100$ is deposited in a bank?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The bank has to keep $\$5$ of the deposit on reserve, but it can loan out all $\$100$ from the bond sale.
The reserve requirement for bond sales is half of the requirement for deposits.
The bank must keep all proceeds from a bond sale at the central bank.
The bank has to pay the federal funds rate on the deposit.
The bank has to pay the discount rate on the deposit.
